feature: Words whitespace comments newlines and basic shell operators
notes: Lexer covers common POSIX token boundaries and comments.
Includes < > >> >| << <<- <& >& <> and io-number forms.

feature: Single quotes double quotes and backslash quoting
notes: Single quotes, double quotes, backslash quoting, and escaped-newline continuation have representative corpus coverage; deeper recursive contexts remain.

A backslash followed by newline is removed before token interpretation in unquoted and double-quoted representative contexts; kept out of the differential corpus because its runner decodes literal \n escapes.
Parser preserves nested CST spans and executor expands common cases.
Supported with POSIX backslash fixes but legacy nesting edge cases remain.

feature: Alias substitution timing and reserved-word interaction
notes: Key behavior slices are covered separately; full parser-level token state and exact substitution timing remain partial.
Command-position aliases recursively expand while active aliases do not recurse indefinitely.

feature: Alias values ending in blank keep the next word eligible for alias substitution
notes: Trailing blank continuation is covered by POSIX corpus and REPL unit coverage.

feature: Aliases defined by an earlier complete command affect later commands
notes: Non-interactive chunk execution applies alias definitions only to later complete commands.
CST has here_doc_body nodes and lowering extracts ordered bodies; exact POSIX token timing still has edges.

feature: Simple command assignments arguments and redirections
notes: Assignment prefixes and redirections are lowered into execution IR.
Covers && || semicolon and newline sequencing.
Real pipe execution exists for external and mixed stages.
External builtin and compound async commands create waitable background jobs.
Common forms execute; strict diagnostics still incomplete for all malformed forms.

Loop execution break/continue and redirected read conditions have baseline coverage, with dash-compatible loop/case smoke cases. Additional dash-style loop-control smoke coverage exercises continue and break through case arms.
POSIX word-list form exists and strict diagnostics reject missing or invalid loop variables; Bash arithmetic for is outside POSIX baseline.

feature: case command and case item CST
notes: Supported spec-clause rows cover pattern lists, optional leading parenthesis, empty arms, final unterminated arm, no-match status, nested case bodies, reserved-word subject handling, and malformed diagnostics; this coarse planning row stays baseline to avoid over-claiming broader CST/token completeness.

Function bodies are lowered when definitions are registered; deeper nested-definition and declaration edge cases remain. Additional dash-style return status smoke coverage exercises early function return.
State isolation status propagation and redirections work for common cases.
Executes in current shell with fd redirection support.
Explicit strict mode exists but grammar coverage is narrow.
Tilde expansion for ~ and ~/path using HOME has corpus coverage; ~user and assignment-word contexts remain.

feature: Basic parameter expansion and assignment/default operators
notes: Many POSIX operators exist; diagnostics and nested edge cases remain.
Supports POSIX removal forms for common patterns.
Diagnostic word expansion, parameter-specific stderr, and non-interactive exit are covered for unset and null parameters, including unquoted multi-word diagnostic words.
Positionals and current status/background pid are modeled.
Global positional parameters can be set and cleared with set --; function-local positional behavior and quoted/unquoted expansion rows are split separately.

feature: Command substitution expansion and field behavior
notes: Command substitution execution, field behavior, and trailing-newline trimming are covered; strict unterminated substitution diagnostics are split into supported rows.
Command substitution removes trailing newlines while preserving embedded newlines in representative quoted assignments.

feature: Arithmetic expansion with variables assignments and operators
notes: Bash-like nonnumeric value handling is not fully POSIX-differential safe.
IFS-aware splitting covers whitespace trimming, non-whitespace, adjacent non-whitespace delimiters, generated empty field removal, and empty IFS; remaining POSIX edge cases stay baseline.

Sorted globbing, slash components, dotfile rules, unmatched literal behavior, escaped metacharacters, and basic bracket expressions are covered; locale-specific collation remains outside Rush's bytewise matching model.
Quote removal, quoted empty field preservation, and escaped-newline removal have representative corpus coverage; recursive contexts remain baseline.

feature: Input output append and noclobber redirections
notes: Uses real fd/file operations for execution paths. Additional BusyBox ash-style append-order smoke coverage verifies repeated append redirections.
Shell-visible fd tracking handles common cases.
Creates missing files and avoids truncating existing files.

feature: Here-doc expansion quoted delimiters and tab stripping
notes: Safe fd materialization and parser body nodes exist; exact parser integration still evolving.

feature: Real fd redirections for builtins functions and groups
notes: Inherited-stdio paths use real fd save/restore; capture paths still model output in memory.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU 2.8.1 Consequences of Shell Errors
notes: Diagnostics exist but POSIX special-builtin/non-interactive consequences need stricter modeling.

feature: Initial environment import and assignment prefixes
notes: Environment import, startup IFS/PPID/PWD initialization, and command-prefix assignment semantics exist.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU cd and pwd utilities
notes: Startup PWD validation, OLDPWD, cd -, and CDPATH updates exist; physical mode and symlink normalization remain explicit follow-up work.
Readonly assignment rejection and readonly -p listing have corpus coverage.
umask -S prints user, group, and other permission bits derived from the current mask.
unset without -f removes a variable while leaving a same-name function in place.
unset defaults to variable removal and supports explicit -v and -f modes.

feature: POSIX special builtin classification and assignment persistence
notes: Classification exists; failure consequences need deeper POSIX modeling.
Implemented as special builtin.
Current-shell effects and PATH search for slashless operands have corpus evidence; non-readable file behavior remains.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU dot utility; XCU 2.8.1 Consequences of Shell Errors
notes: Missing file operands and nonexistent dot scripts report diagnostics and stop non-interactive execution as special-builtin failures.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU break and continue utilities
notes: Loop control works in loops, including nested break/continue levels; diagnostic edge cases are split separately.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU cd and pwd utilities
notes: Logical pwd, cd -, CDPATH behavior, cd -L/-P behavior, and pwd -L/-P behavior have corpus coverage; deeper symlink normalization and cd path diagnostics remain.
Useful lookup modes, -p, lookup suppression, -- option termination, and function lookup suppression exist; full POSIX edge cases remain.
command executes the utility/builtin path rather than a shell function with the same name.
command accepts -- to terminate option parsing for execution and lookup modes.
Unsupported options, missing lookup operands, and excess lookup operands report diagnostics and leave non-interactive execution running as regular builtin failures.
Supports environment printing, -i, -- option termination, name=value operands, and command execution under a modified environment.
env accepts -- after -i and before assignment operands.
Unsupported options report diagnostics, and missing env utility operands propagate command-not-found status while leaving non-interactive execution running.
Common eval behavior, argument concatenation, empty eval status, and special assignment persistence have corpus coverage; diagnostics edge cases remain.

feature: eval argument concatenation and empty status
notes: eval concatenates arguments for execution and empty eval returns success.
Real replacement exists for safe inherited external command path.
Explicit status behavior and default previous-status behavior are covered.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU return utility; XCU 2.8.1 Consequences of Shell Errors
notes: Non-interactive return outside a function, invalid numeric operands, and excess operands diagnose and stop execution with status 2.
exit without an operand exits with the previous command status.
return uses its explicit numeric operand or the previous command status when no operand is supplied.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU export and unset utilities
notes: Export baseline, export -p and readonly -p listing, unset default variable mode, unset -v variables, and unset -f functions are covered.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU export and readonly utilities
notes: export -p and readonly -p expose marked variables in a reusable listing form for simple values.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU export and readonly utilities

notes: Invalid variable names, readonly modification attempts, unsupported options, and -p excess operands diagnose and stop non-interactive execution for representative export, readonly, and unset operands.
Clusters, required arguments, silent missing arguments, explicit arg operands, -- termination, and validation diagnostics are covered; remaining OPTIND edge cases stay baseline.
Invalid optstrings and invalid variable names report diagnostics and leave non-interactive execution running as regular builtin failures.
unalias -a clears the alias table.

feature: printf formats escapes width and precision
notes: Broad baseline exists including format reuse, b/c conversions, escapes, numeric formats, width, and precision; full POSIX format grammar remains.
Missing format operands, malformed formats, unsupported conversions, and invalid numeric operands report diagnostics and leave non-interactive execution running as regular builtin failures.
Backslash, -r, -- option termination, custom IFS, empty IFS, last-variable assignment, EOF status, and unsupported option behavior are covered; more IFS edge cases remain.

Many common predicates exist, including regular/directory/string/integer, symlink, terminal, mode-bit, special file type checks, and !/-a/-o/grouping expression grammar; remaining precedence/ambiguity edge cases remain.
Uses getrusage when libc is available with fallback elsewhere.

feature: Trap listing EXIT and common signal traps
notes: Common catchable signal dispatch exists; signal semantics remain limited.
Basic get/set behavior, -S symbolic output, and representative symbolic mask operands are covered.
umask accepts representative symbolic assignment operands using u/g/o/a classes and r/w/x permissions.
Invalid numeric and symbolic masks, unsupported options, and excess operands report diagnostics while allowing subsequent commands to run.
Tracked pids are waitable; full job specs and stopped jobs are pending.
Invalid pid operands and unknown pids report diagnostics and leave non-interactive execution running as regular builtin failures.

posix_ref: POSIX.1-2024 XCU alias and unalias utilities
notes: Builtin list/remove operations, unalias -a, and some parser interaction covered; recursive timing edge cases remain.

No-operand table plus -l -p, numeric/%, current/previous job operands, current/previous markers, and nonblocking status refresh exist.

notes: Interactive handler exists; terminal/editor interactions are still evolving.
